Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. AyaFlow: A high-performance, eBPF-based network traffic analyzer written in Rust 72 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Kyushu Railway Company Train Varieties 36 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 164 upvotes, 21 comments
  4. More on Version Control 38 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. The road signs that teach travellers about France 25 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Show HN: QuickBEAM – run JavaScript as supervised Erlang/OTP processes 61 upvotes, 10 comments
  7. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 100 upvotes, 23 comments
  8. Sky Wins Irish Court Order to Unmask 300 Pirate IPTV Users via Revolut Bank 42 upvotes, 9 comments
  9. Full network of clitoral nerves mapped out for first time 203 upvotes, 67 comments
  10. Ohm's Peg-to-WASM Compiler 21 upvotes, 3 comments
  11. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 313 upvotes, 120 comments
  12. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 340 upvotes, 143 comments
  13. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 483 upvotes, 213 comments
  14. The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history 59 upvotes, 19 comments
  15. Neovim 0.12.0 251 upvotes, 111 comments
  16. The Cognitive Dark Forest 159 upvotes, 76 comments
  17. Show HN: Crazierl – An Erlang Operating System 25 upvotes, 7 comments
  18. A nearly perfect USB cable tester 260 upvotes, 141 comments
  19. Creating West Coast Buddhism (2024) 38 upvotes, 14 comments
  20. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 558 upvotes, 333 comments
  21. Observations from carbon dioxide monitoring 37 upvotes, 15 comments
  22. I'll buy your electronics to feed our robot 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Coding Agents Could Make Free Software Matter Again 34 upvotes, 17 comments
  24. ChatGPT Won't Let You Type Until Cloudflare Reads Your React State 227 upvotes, 165 comments
  25. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 268 upvotes, 200 comments
  26. The Epistemology of Microphysics 36 upvotes, 21 comments
  27. C++26 is done ISO C++ standards meeting, Trip Report 139 upvotes, 108 comments
  28. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 51 upvotes, 40 comments
  29. There is No Spoon. A software engineers primer for demystified ML 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Show HN: I made a "programming language" looking for feedback 21 upvotes, 19 comments

Last generated 7:00am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
